input.title: Commercial Lease Administration Platform
input.description: Commercial Lease Administration Platform is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with Next.js and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, PostgreSQL, Elasticsearch for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Property Portfolio Registration
requirements: Users must be able to complete property portfolio registration through the approved entry points provided by the platform.
The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing property portfolio registration to continue.
Property Portfolio Registration must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
The user interface for property portfolio registration should provide clear success, failure, and recovery messages for common scenarios.

FR-02.title: Tenant Organization Onboarding
requirements: The system must support tenant organization onboarding as a complete business workflow within the application.
Tenant Organization Onboarding should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with tenant organization onboarding from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to tenant organization onboarding for operational support.

FR-03.title: Lease Abstract Data Capture
requirements: The system must support lease abstract data capture as a complete business workflow within the application.
Lease Abstract Data Capture should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with lease abstract data capture from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to lease abstract data capture for operational support.

FR-04.title: Rent Schedule Management
requirements: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete rent schedule management according to the approved business workflow.
The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving rent schedule management changes.
Rent Schedule Management must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
The interface should surface the current workflow state, next available actions, and blocking conditions for rent schedule management.

FR-05.title: Escalation Clause Tracking
requirements: The system must capture escalation clause tracking data in real time or near real time as operational events occur.
Escalation Clause Tracking should provide a timeline, status history, or change log so users can understand what happened and when.
Users must be able to review, filter, and reconcile escalation clause tracking records against the related business entities.
The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during escalation clause tracking.

FR-06.title: Security Deposit Monitoring
requirements: Users must be able to complete security deposit monitoring through the approved entry points provided by the platform.
The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing security deposit monitoring to continue.
Security Deposit Monitoring must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
The user interface for security deposit monitoring should provide clear success, failure, and recovery messages for common scenarios.

FR-07.title: Lease Document Repository
requirements: The system must provide end-to-end lease document repository capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records.
Lease Document Repository should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate.
Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to lease document repository without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within lease document repository and retain operational history for reporting.

FR-08.title: Renewal Notice Automation
requirements: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete renewal notice automation according to the approved business workflow.
The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving renewal notice automation changes.
Renewal Notice Automation must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
The interface should surface the current workflow state, next available actions, and blocking conditions for renewal notice automation.

FR-09.title: Critical Date Calendar
requirements: The system must support critical date calendar as a complete business workflow within the application.
Critical Date Calendar should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with critical date calendar from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to critical date calendar for operational support.

requirements: The system must support maintenance charge allocation as a complete business workflow within the application.

FR-11.title: Invoice Generation for Tenants
requirements: The system must support invoice generation for tenants as a complete business workflow within the application.
Invoice Generation for Tenants should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with invoice generation for tenants from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to invoice generation for tenants for operational support.

FR-12.title: Payment Reconciliation
requirements: The system must capture payment reconciliation data in real time or near real time as operational events occur.
Payment Reconciliation should provide a timeline, status history, or change log so users can understand what happened and when.
Users must be able to review, filter, and reconcile payment reconciliation records against the related business entities.
The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during payment reconciliation.

FR-13.title: Vacancy and Occupancy Dashboard
requirements: The system must provide vacancy and occupancy dashboard with role-appropriate filters, date ranges, and drill-down views.
Vacancy and Occupancy Dashboard should calculate metrics from current transactional data and keep summaries aligned with source records.
Authorized users must be able to export or share vacancy and occupancy dashboard outputs in formats used by business teams.
The platform should retain query history, report configuration, or dashboard preferences where that improves repeated analysis.

FR-14.title: Amendment Version Tracking
requirements: The system must capture amendment version tracking data in real time or near real time as operational events occur.
Amendment Version Tracking should provide a timeline, status history, or change log so users can understand what happened and when.
Users must be able to review, filter, and reconcile amendment version tracking records against the related business entities.
The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during amendment version tracking.

requirements: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete landlord approval workflow according to the approved business workflow.
The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving landlord approval workflow changes.
Landlord Approval Workflow must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.

The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during broker commission tracking.

FR-17.title: Portfolio Reporting for Finance
requirements: The system must provide portfolio reporting for finance with role-appropriate filters, date ranges, and drill-down views.
Portfolio Reporting for Finance should calculate metrics from current transactional data and keep summaries aligned with source records.
Authorized users must be able to export or share portfolio reporting for finance outputs in formats used by business teams.
The platform should retain query history, report configuration, or dashboard preferences where that improves repeated analysis.

FR-18.title: Audit Trail for Lease Changes
requirements: The system must capture audit trail for lease changes data in real time or near real time as operational events occur.
Audit Trail for Lease Changes should provide a timeline, status history, or change log so users can understand what happened and when.
Users must be able to review, filter, and reconcile audit trail for lease changes records against the related business entities.
The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during audit trail for lease changes.

FR-01.title: Design Property Portfolio Registration UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for property portfolio registration. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for property portfolio registration is complete and reviewable.
The property portfolio registration design is responsive and accessible.
The design for property portfolio registration aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of Property Portfolio Registration
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for property portfolio registration. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Users must be able to complete property portfolio registration through the approved entry points provided by the platform; The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing property portfolio registration to continue; Property Portfolio Registration must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required property portfolio registration user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for property portfolio registration are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for property portfolio registration is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of Property Portfolio Registration
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for property portfolio registration. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Users must be able to complete property portfolio registration through the approved entry points provided by the platform; The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing property portfolio registration to continue; Property Portfolio Registration must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for property portfolio registration is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for property portfolio registration enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for property portfolio registration is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Security Hardening for Property Portfolio Registration
FR-01.description: Implement the security controls required for property portfolio registration. Cover authentication, authorization, encryption, auditing, abuse prevention, and compliance checks where the SRS or workflow calls for them.
acceptance_criteria: Security controls for property portfolio registration enforce the required authentication, authorization, or data-protection rules.
Sensitive operations and failure scenarios for property portfolio registration are logged, validated, and protected against misuse.
The implementation of property portfolio registration satisfies the relevant privacy, audit, or compliance expectations in the SRS.

FR-01.title: DevOps Setup for Property Portfolio Registration
FR-01.description: Set up the operational support for property portfolio registration. Configure deployment settings, secrets, background jobs, monitoring, alerting, backup, restore, or recovery controls needed for the feature to run safely in target environments.
acceptance_criteria: Operational setup for property portfolio registration covers deployment, configuration, and environment readiness.
Monitoring, alerting, backup, or recovery controls relevant to property portfolio registration are in place and verifiable.
The runtime behavior of property portfolio registration can be observed, supported, and recovered in target environments.

FR-01.title: Testing Property Portfolio Registration Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for property portfolio registration. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for property portfolio registration.
Security, validation, and permission checks for property portfolio registration are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important property portfolio registration behaviors.

Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: The system must provide portfolio reporting for finance with role-appropriate filters, date ranges, and drill-down views; Portfolio Reporting for Finance should calculate metrics from current transactional data and keep summaries aligned with source records; Authorized users must be able to export or share portfolio reporting for finance outputs in formats used by business teams.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required portfolio reporting for finance user interactions.
